rulings.Case Title: IVAN MERL BARNEY VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Responses To Deere Companys Specially Prepared Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 29, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Deere & Company¿s motion to compel plaintiffs¿ further responses to defendant¿s special interrogatory no. 9 is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiffs¿ initial failure to timely serve a response precludes plaintiffs from asserting objections so within 5 days plaintiffs must serve an amended response that does not include objections. No further substantive response is required. The response served by plaintiffs on January 16, 2013 is sufficient. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $2500. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LEWIS WISECARVER VS. AMCORD, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Pierce Enterprises To Produce Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And Responsive Documents, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 29, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant Pierce Enterprises to produce custodian(s) of records and person(s) most knowledgeable for deposition and responsive documents and request for sanctions of $1081 is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
